rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/26/2022 1) Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 2) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 3) Please file receipts and a bank statement showing the overpayment of fees have been paid back. This is now the second account in which there has been issues with overpayment of fees. Please explain what steps if any have been taken to stop this issue. Please explain whether the trust is be entitled to lost interest. 4) Please verify the balance of assets. [See LRSF 14.35 D.] 5) Please file timesheets for the trustee. [See LRSF 14.53]. 6) Please explain the entries on Schedule A-1 which have no description. 7) Please file a receipt for repairs replace roof on house for $11,400.00. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 1/10/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SHOSTAC LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Request for trustee fees a. Trustee requests $60/hr. Non-professional fiduciaries are generally granted up to $45/hr. b. Trustee requests fees for petitioning to be appointed conservator over the person. c. Petitioner requests fees for what appears to be non-trustee services. d. 32 hours were spent in response to Court inquiry about actions she took, compiling records of invoices and time spent to care for Eugene and Eda which were paid by the trust. 2) Request for attorney fees a. Attorney already spent 62 hours (per 10/27/20 declaration supporting 1st account, totaling $23,906) to prepare the first accounting petition (which excluded the actual accounting that was prepared by a third party), for which attorney was already compensated for. Attorney now alleges that he spent an additional 19.25 hours on that same accounting petition (invoice dated 11/5/20) and requests an additional $7,343 for those services. Attorney invoice dated 1/4/21 also included work related to the first accounting, including to respond to Examiner's Notes (~$7k, based on combined rate). b. Other questionable entries: i. Charges for communicating with the bank, the accounting preparer and what looks to be the bookkeeper, which appears should have been performed by the trustee. ii. Inputting trust expenses into a spreadsheet. iii. Interoffice communications regarding update of status. iv. One hour to draft a letter to the probate department re: courtesy copies. v. Three total hours travel time to FedEx and Post Office. vi. Determining who is authorized to provide burial instructions, which does not appear to be the role of the trustee's attorney. 3) Court to determine whether court supervision should be terminated. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 10th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NANCY MAZZA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, as no further objection has been filed, although the executor and her attorney are incorrect about the importance of filed papers being available for viewing: due process rights, in fact, necessitate interested parties having access to the documents filed and to be considered by the Court. Furthermore, there was no mention of liabilities or costs in the third amended petition. The summary of account attached as Exhibit B to the supplement filed on 11/29/21 also begins with two figures that do not correspond with the property on hand or receipts set forth in paragraph 29 of the third amended petition or on page 4 of this supplement. Assets of the "probate estate" do not necessarily include after-discovered assets, as after-discovered assets are/were not part of the probate estate that was administered. No correct notice of the third amended petition was given, only of the supplement to it.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JEREMIAS ZEULI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/25/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No courtesy copy of the third supplement filed on 11/10/21 has been received yet by the assigned Examiner, as required by LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C. It was reviewed online as a ONE-TIME courtesy in hopes of avoiding another continuance. Original (or original electronic) bank statements must be filed to verify the BEGINNING balances. [Probate Code Sect. 2620(c)(2).] The accounting still does not have a schedule showing the MARKET value of property on hand. [Prob. C. Sect. 1063(a).] Paragraph 5 of the third supplement merely repeats the property on hand listed in the supplement filed on 10/22/21 and, although it says that it is the "market" value, the total non-cash assets is shown as the "(carry value)" in that same paragraph. A SCHEDULE showing the liabilities must be filed, not merely an approximation. A copy of the reverse mortgage statement must be filed to confirm the balance. CRC 7.751(b) refers to CRC 7.702 only for the purpose of having a fee request supported, not because "extraordinary" fees are being requested. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/14/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WES LEE WINTERS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/25/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The estate should be distributed in eight equal shares at the nibling level, as the two sisters who had issue predeceased, making their children, all of the same degree of kinship to the decedent, equally entitled to the estate. The issue of the three predeceased niblings take 1/5 or 1/3 or 1/4, as the case may be, of their parent's 1/8 interest. [Probate Code Sections 6402(c) and 240.] A general pecuniary bequest not made within one year from the date of death bears interest. [Probate Code Sections 12001 and 12003.] Courtesy copies of the amendment to the petition and proof of service, plus a revised proposed order, must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/14/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B. (Bank statements are not needed when the accounting has been waived. Many of the waivers of account, however, were not properly executed (not dated). It would also have been helpful if the waivers of account had been submitted in the same order as the heirs are listed in the petition. Assets are shown on p. 8 of the petition, line 3, to include tangible personal property, but none was inventoried. Since none is proposed to be distributed, this will be overlooked.)

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF ANN CRONIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: FILING OF STATUS REPORT
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/26/2022. Notice shows conservatee died 10/01/2021. Although there has been a waiver of account the final report is still to be filed. Final accounts where the conservatorship has been terminated by the death of the conservatee. Final accounts must be filed within 90 days of the death of a conservatee. Where there has been a waiver of accounts or a deferral of court assessment fees, the conservator of the estate must file a final report stating the current circumstances of the estate re: the need for a final account or the possibility of payment of the assessment fee. When the Court learns that a conservatee has died and no final account or report has been filed, the Court will set a status date for filing of the final account or report. [See LRSF 14.48 I. 5)]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAM MICHAEL HENRY REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For An Order To Confirm Validity Of Trust; Confirm Trustee; And Confirm Trustee; And Confirm Assets To Revocable Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/15/2022 by the assigned judicial officer after review. 1) This will be the last continuance granted to allow proper service on Rebecca Oden. If substituted service becomes necessary again, a proof of service must show full compliance with CCP section 415.20(b), including the process server's declaration of mailing at least 40 days before the next hearing. 2) Counsel for the petitioner and objector must file a joint status statement and deliver a courtesy copy to the assigned examiner by 02/08/2022. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LANA HAMILTON BAILEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/26/2022. 1) The bank statement attached to the Supplement filed 11/24/2021 shows the ending balance in account ending #6914 as $208,149.28. The balance in the account shows $204,288.78. Please explain. [See LRSF 14.35 D.] 2) Please file account statements for account ending in #6573 in accordance with LRSF 14.35 D. 3) Examiner received an unfiled bank statement which has been left for you to pickup in room 103 no later than 12/22/2021 or the documents will be recycled. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 4) The proposed order distribution does not match what is requested in the verified supplement. The proposed order should state the net amount of cash to be distributed to each beneficiary. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 1/10/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CARL WILHELM FOLK LIVING TRUST DATED MAY 3, 2002
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Title To Trust Property
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/25/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: This does not appear to be the proper venue, as the trustee is in Palm Desert, not San Francisco. [Probate Code Sect. 17002.] If this is the proper venue, the following issues will need to be addressed: (1) since Probate Code Sect. 850 succeeded Heggstad, all heirs must be identified, who would be entitled to the estate if the trust were not valid, and if they are not trust beneficiaries, they must receive personal service of notice; (2) a copy of the Schwab statement immediately preceding the date of death must be filed to confirm how title to the account was vested; and (3) the proof of service of the Notice of Hearing filed on 12/6/21 (no courtesy provided, as required by LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.) showed that notice was mailed on 11/29/21 for the hearing on 12/21/21, which was not at least 30 days' notice. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/14/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE REVOCABLE TRUST DECLARATION OF FRANK H. YUEN AND ANNIE C. YUEN U/D/T/ MARCH 23, 1994
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Final Distribution Of Trust Estate And For Payment Of Fees And Costs To Petitioner'S Attorney
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The petitioner alleges that she became entitled to outright distribution of her share of the trust in 2015 when the surviving settlor died, but the trustees have distributed only 22% of the share to the petitioner. The trustees object to the petitioner's valuation and request a 90-day continuance for a 6-year accounting and final distribution to be completed before April 2022. 2) A complete copy of the governing trust instrument has not been filed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.
